A real data application is discussed in the FLR setting
The data set consists of 3000 bitcoin addresses with 501 equally spaced observations of cumulative credit.
Cumulative credit at each point in time is used as the explanatory variable X for predicting the binary outcome Y.
X represents the cumulative credit curves and Y is the indicator variable for whether an address is associated with illegal activities.
A model is proposed using the log-likelihood of the negative binomial to predict the probability of Y being 1 given X.
The log-likelihood of the negative binomial can be included in the equation alongside other classification loss functions for optimization.
